As far as we have knowledge of God within us, and the favor of God toward us, our light has come. And if God's glory is seen upon us to our honor, we ought, not only with our lips, but in our lives, to return its praise. We find nothing in the history of the Jews which can be considered a fulfillment of the prophecy in this chapter; we must conclude it relates principally to future events. It predicts the purity and enlargement of the church. The conversion of souls is described here. They come to Christ, to the church, to the word and ordinances, as doves to their own home; they fly there for refuge and shelter, they fly there for rest. What a pleasant sight it is to see poor souls hastening to Christ!

God will be very gracious. We must begin with his promise, from which all mercies arise. Many shall be brought into the church, even from far countries. Christ is always ready to receive all who come to him; and the gate of mercy is always open, night and day. All those around the church shall be made serviceable to it. But those who will not be subject to Christ's golden scepter, to his word and Spirit, who will not be kept in by the laws and rules of his family, shall be broken in pieces by his iron rod. The peculiar advantages of every nation, and of every description of men, shall join to beautify the church of Christ. We must suppose this to be accomplished in the beauties of holiness, and the graces and comforts of the Spirit, with which gospel ordinances are adorned and enriched. Blessed be his name, the gates of Zion are ever open to returning sinners.

We must look for the full accomplishment in times and things beyond those of the Old Testament church. The nations and their kings shall work for the good of the church. Such a salvation, such a redemption, will be worked out for you, as reveals itself to be the work of the Lord. Everything shall be changed for the better. In your land there shall no more be heard threats from those who do violence, nor complaints from those who suffer violence. Your walls shall be means of safety, your gates shall be written upon with praises to God. In the close of this chapter are images and expressions used in the description of the New Jerusalem; 22:5. Nothing can correspond to this but some future glorious state of the church on earth, or the state of the church triumphant in heaven. Those who make God their only light shall have him as their all-sufficient light. And happiness shall know no change or alloy. No people on earth are all righteous; but there are no mixtures in heaven. They will be wholly righteous. The spirits of just men will be made perfect there. The glory of the church will be to the honor of God. When it is finished, it will appear a work of wonder. It may seem too difficult to bring about, but the God of almighty power has undertaken it. It may seem to be delayed and put off; but the Lord will hasten it in the time appointed by his wisdom, though not in the time prescribed by our folly. Let this hope cheer us under all difficulties, and stir us up to all diligence, that we may have an abundant entrance into this everlasting kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
